eventMetadata.context_description: Brad Lander's commanding trader consensus at 84% implied probability in the NY-10 Democratic primary reflects private polls showing him with a sizable lead over incumbent Dan Goldman, bolstered by recent progressive endorsements including 32BJ SEIU on April 2 and backing from Mayor Zohran Mamdani. Goldman's 16% share stems from his fundraising dominance, including a April 23 pledge to match donations with at least $1 million personally amid criticisms of attempting to buy the race. The June 23 primary pits Lander's grassroots relationships against Goldman's resources in this Manhattan-Brooklyn district, with minor candidates like Alexa Avilés trailing far behind; late shifts could arise from debates or further union moves.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the CA-26 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the CA-26 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices a Democratic victory at 93.5% in California's 26th Congressional District House race, driven by the district's Solid Democratic rating from Cook Political Report (D+9 partisan lean) and similar assessments from Inside Elections and Sabato's Crystal Ball, where Kamala Harris carried 56% in 2024. Incumbent Julia Brownley's January retirement opened the Ventura County-based seat, but five Democrats—including fundraising leader state Sen. Jacqui Irwin with nearly $500,000 cash on hand—crowd a June 2 top-two primary against four Republicans, positioning the general election on November 3 as a probable Democrat-vs-Democrat or safe Democratic hold. No district-specific polls exist, but forecasters align with historical Democratic dominance. A Republican upset would require consolidating their primary vote for a top-two berth amid a national midterm wave, scandal hitting frontrunners, or unexpectedly low Democratic turnout.

markets.question: Theo FDV above $1B one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Theo's token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Theo (https://x.com/Theo_Network) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Anysphere (Cursor) IPO before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed company completes an Initial Public Offering (IPO) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, as confirmed by official company announcements or credible news sources. The IPO refers to the first sale of stock by the listed company to the public on any recognized stock exchange.

If the listed company merges with another entity, is acquired, or ceases to exist before the market resolves, the market will also resolve to "No".

This market will resolve early if the listed company completes an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

The resolution source for this market is a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment hinges on the reopening IPO window for AI and space tech giants, catalyzed by SpaceX's April 1 confidential filing for a blockbuster listing potentially valued at $1.75 trillion and OpenAI's groundwork for a second-half 2026 debut targeting $1 trillion-plus. Anthropic narrowed the revenue gap with OpenAI per April reports, fueling early 2026 IPO speculation, while Databricks secured $1.8 billion in debt financing in January to support its public plans amid data analytics demand. Fintechs like Stripe, Klarna, and Chime are testing waters despite valuation resets. Upcoming S-1 filings, Q2 earnings, and Federal Reserve rate decisions could trigger rapid shifts, though economic headwinds or AI hype fatigue pose delays.

markets.question: Will Brighton finish in 3rd place in the 2025-26 English Premier League?
markets.description:
This is a polymarket on whether the listed club will finish in 3rd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed club finishes 3rd in the final standings of the 2025–26 English Premier League. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

If at any point it becomes impossible for the listed club to finish 3rd in the 2025-26 EPL season (e.g. they are mathematically eliminated), the market will resolve to "No".

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed by October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.title: English Premier League – 3rd Place
events.description: This is a polymarket to predict which club will finish in 3rd place in the 2025–26 English Premier League (soccer).

eventMetadata.context_description: Manchester United hold a commanding three-point lead in third place with 61 points from 34 games, bolstered by their hard-fought 2-1 victory over Brentford on April 27—Casemiro and Benjamin Sesko scoring in a crucial win that extended their edge over Liverpool and Aston Villa, both on 58 points but separated by goal difference (+13 vs. +5). Traders' 70% consensus on United reflects this buffer, superior GD, and a pivotal upcoming head-to-head at Anfield against Liverpool, alongside a manageable run-in favoring Champions League qualification. Liverpool's pursuit faces headwinds from Mohamed Salah's hamstring injury sustained April 25 versus Crystal Palace, while Villa's inferior GD and recent draw-heavy form temper their 8.8% chances in the tight top-four race.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Atlanta be between 68-69°F on May 2?
markets.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects National Weather Service forecasts pinning Atlanta's May 2 high temperature in the mid-60s°F range, driven by a frontal boundary ushering clouds, scattered showers, and lighter winds that suppress peak heating below the early May climatological average of 79°F. Recent model ensembles from GFS and ECMWF show tight clustering around 64-67°F, with 64-65°F and 66-67°F each at 19.5% implied probability, differentiated primarily by subtle variations in morning rain timing and afternoon cloud persistence—earlier clearance could nudge toward 67°F, while prolonged overcast favors 64°F. Ongoing drought relief from late-April storms sets a cooler baseline, but new 12Z model runs and NWS updates by midday May 1 could refine these odds amid inherent short-range forecast uncertainty.

markets.question: Will annual inflation increase by 4.0% in April?
markets.description:
This is a market about inflation over the 12-month period ending April 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

This market will resolve to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending in April 2026 according to the monthly Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) report.

The resolution source for this market will be the BLS Consumer Price Index report released for April 2026 (https://www.bls.gov/bls/news-release/cpi.htm), currently scheduled to be released on May 12, 2026, at 8:30 AM ET. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly BLS CPI news release, which reports inflation over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g., 2.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the BLS does not release the relevant figures on the scheduled date, this market may remain open up until the scheduled release time of the next CPI report (https://www.bls.gov/schedule). If the information is not released by that time, this market will resolve according to the figures of the most recent previous month with available data.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a modest 33.7% implied probability for April 2026 CPI year-over-year at 3.7%, with 3.6% (21.0%) and 3.8% (25.9%) in close contention, reflecting tight uncertainty over energy persistence following March's hotter-than-expected 3.3% print driven by a 10.9% energy surge amid Iran-related geopolitical tensions. Cleveland Fed nowcast as of April 29 holds at 3.56%, tempered by softer core CPI at 2.56%, but sticky shelter costs and potential base effects from last year's declines introduce upside risks favoring 3.7-3.8%. Gasoline volatility remains the key differentiator, with the May 12 BLS release poised to influence FOMC rate cut expectations amid Treasury yields hovering near 4.5%.

events.title: Al Okhdood SC vs. Al Ettifaq Saudi Club
events.description: This event is for the upcoming Saudi Professional League game, scheduled for Thursday, April 30, 2026 between Al Okhdood SC and Al Ettifaq Saudi Club.

eventMetadata.context_description: Al Ettifaq's mid-table security at 7th with 42 points from 29 Saudi Pro League matches contrasts sharply with Al Okhdood's relegation fight from rock-bottom 17th on just 16 points, driving trader consensus to price the visitors at 51% implied probability despite the away fixture at Prince Hathloul Stadium. Recent head-to-head dominance—Al Ettifaq's three straight wins, including 2-0 in January—bolsters their edge, while Al Okhdood's dismal form (four losses in last five, no clean sheet in nine) and leaky defense (61 goals conceded) temper home advantage. Al Ettifaq's mixed results (two losses, one win lately) and Jack Hendry's suspension add draw risk at 25%, but quality gap favors the Commandos.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 96.7% for Putin and Zelenskyy shaking hands by June 30, reflecting entrenched diplomatic deadlock amid ongoing Russia-Ukraine military escalations, including recent Ukrainian drone strikes on Russian oil infrastructure and Russian advances on multiple fronts. Despite Zelenskyy's April 25 openness to a summit in Azerbaijan and Kremlin statements that Putin would meet only to finalize peace agreements—citing absent political will from Kyiv—no direct talks are scheduled, with preconditions like territorial concessions dividing the leaders. Realistic shifts could arise from a sudden breakthrough in Geneva-format negotiations or a major battlefield reversal before the deadline.

markets.question: Madrid Open: Jannik Sinner vs Arthur Fils
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Jannik Sinner and Arthur Fils in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for May 1, 2026 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Jannik Sinner' if Jannik Sinner advances against Arthur Fils.

This market will resolve to 'Arthur Fils' if Arthur Fils advances against Jannik Sinner.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: World No. 1 Jannik Sinner, riding a 26-match win streak at Masters 1000 events and perfect 9-0 on clay in 2026, advanced to the Madrid Open semifinals with a straight-sets quarterfinal victory over local wildcard Rafael Jodar on Wednesday. Challenger Arthur Fils, world No. 25 and also 9-0 on clay this year, extended his nine-match winning run by overpowering Jiri Lehecka 6-3, 6-4, showcasing aggressive baseline play suited to the high-altitude clay courts. Sinner leads their head-to-head 1-0 from hard-court meetings, but Fils' red-hot form and improved clay movement pose an upset threat in Friday's matchup, with trader consensus heavily favoring the top seed's experience and serving prowess amid no reported injuries for either.

markets.question: Will Rafael López Aliaga win the first round of the 2026 Peruvian presidential elections by at least 15%?
markets.description:
First-round presidential elections are scheduled to be held in Peru on April 12, 2026, with a potential second round on June 7, 2026, if no candidate receives more than 50% of the valid votes outright.

This market will resolve according to the margin of victory between the top two candidates in the first round of the 2026 Peruvian presidential elections.

For the purpose of this market, the “margin of victory” is defined as the absolute difference between the percentages of valid votes received by the first-place and second-place candidates. Percentages of the valid votes received by each candidate will be determined by dividing the total number of valid votes each of the top two candidates receives by the sum of all valid votes cast in the election.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If two candidates receive the exact same highest number of valid votes and both are listed, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket for the tied candidate whose last name comes first alphabetically. If only one of the tied candidates is listed, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket for that listed candidate. If neither tied candidate is listed, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the official vote count once the count has been made official.

If the results of the specified election are not known definitively by October 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

This market will resolve based on the results of this election, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve solely on the official results as reported by the Peruvian government, specifically the National Office of Electoral Processes (Oficina Nacional de Procesos Electorales, ONPE) (https://www.onpe.gob.pe/elecciones/) and the National Jury of Elections (Jurado Nacional de Elecciones, JNE) (https://portal.jne.gob.pe/portal/)

If a recount is initiated before the vote total has been made official, the market will remain open until the recount is completed and the vote is made official.

eventMetadata.context_description: Keiko Fujimori's commanding 92.7% implied probability on a first-round margin exceeding 5% stems from official tallies by Peru's National Office of Electoral Processes (ONPE), with over 93% of votes counted as of late April 2026, showing her at 17.1% versus Roberto Sánchez's 12.0% and Rafael López Aliaga's 11.9%—a stable 5.1% edge in a fragmented field of 35 candidates. Pre-election polls from Datum and Ipsos, along with exit surveys, forecasted this lead, bolstered by her strength in Lima and northern regions amid voter fatigue with instability. Logistical delays have slowed final certification, but no major shifts have emerged in recent updates. Challenges could arise from uncounted rural ballots favoring challengers or recounts, though minimal votes remain before the June 7 runoff.

markets.question: Trump renames ICE to NICE by June 30?
markets.description:
On April 27, 2026, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt posted on X that President Donald J. Trump endorsed renaming Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to “NICE.” You can read more about that here: https://x.com/presssec/status/2048746343275938173?s=46.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if Donald Trump signs any federal legislation or performs any executive action that changes the name of Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to “NICE” or to any name that is officially abbreviated as “NICE” by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any executive action or legislation that renames Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to “NICE” or to any name that is officially abbreviated as “NICE” will qualify, regardless of if/when the change goes into effect or if the action is delayed, suspended, or subsequently blocked by judicial or other actions.

This market’s primary resolution source will be official information from the Trump administration; however, a consensus of credible information from major news outlets or government statements will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: President Trump's late April 27 Truth Social endorsement of renaming Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to National Immigration and Customs Enforcement (NICE)—a viral idea from conservative influencers—sparked brief online buzz but no concrete executive or legislative action. Traders' 85.5% implied probability on "No" reflects skepticism over the procedural hurdles: ICE's name is enshrined in the Homeland Security Act of 2002, requiring congressional bill passage through House and Senate committees, floor votes, and presidential signature by June 30, amid competing priorities like DHS budget reconciliation. Absent a formal White House proposal or introduced legislation in the past 48 hours, the wisdom of crowds prices this as a rhetorical PR stunt unlikely to advance swiftly.

markets.question: Will Xi Jinping win the Nobel Peace Prize in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, as announced by the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

If multiple listed individuals or entities jointly receive the prize, the market will resolve to a single winner. If Donald Trump, Volodymyr Zelenskyy,  Benjamin Netanyahu, Vladimir Putin, or Elon Musk are among the recipients, the market will resolve to the highest-ranked among them in that exact order of precedence. If none of those five are among the winners and the prize is awarded jointly to at least one listed individual and at least one listed organization, the market will resolve in favor of the individual. If no prioritized individuals are among the winners and all listed recipients are of the same type (all individuals or all organizations), the market will resolve to the person whose last name, or the entity’s name, comes first in alphabetical order as listed in this market.

If no official announcement regarding the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize has been made by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other."

The resolution source for this market will be the first official announcement from the Norwegian Nobel Committee.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a fragmented field for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize, with Yulia Navalnaya's 8% implied probability narrowly leading Volodymyr Zelenskyy at 7.8% and Donald Trump at 6.5%, underscoring the Norwegian Nobel Committee's unpredictable voting amid diverse global narratives. Zelenskyy's edge stems from his April 1 nomination by University of Oslo professor Dag Øistein Endsjø—praised for Ukrainian resilience against Russian aggression—while Navalnaya sustains momentum through her symbolic dissident role post-Navalny. Trump trails on endorsements like Netanyahu's nod for Middle East diplomacy and Venezuelan opposition leader María Corina Machado's April 18 symbolic prize handover. Differentiators include Zelenskyy's wartime defiance, Navalnaya's human rights advocacy, and Trump's deal-making legacy; a breakthrough peace accord could elevate any frontrunner before October's announcement.

markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be less than $500B at market close on IPO day by December 31, 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2027".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

markets.question: Will Harley-Davidson (HOG) beat quarterly earnings?
markets.description:
As of market creation, Harley-Davidson is estimated to release earnings on May 5, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Harley-Davidson's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.23 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Harley-Davidson reports GAAP EPS greater than $0.23 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Harley-Davidson releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

events.description:
As of market creation, Harley-Davidson is estimated to release earnings on May 5, 2026. The Street consensus estimate for Harley-Davidson's GAAP EPS for the relevant quarter is $0.23 as of market creation. This market will resolve to "Yes" if Harley-Davidson reports GAAP EPS greater than $0.23 for the relevant quarter in its next quarterly earnings release. Otherwise, it will resolve to "No." The resolution source will be the GAAP EPS listed in the company’s official earnings documents. 

If Harley-Davidson releases earnings without GAAP EPS, then the market will resolve according to the GAAP EPS figure reported by SeekingAlpha. If no such figure is published within 96h of market close (4:00:00pm ET) on the day earnings are announced, the market will resolve to “No”.

If the company does not release earnings within 45 calendar days of the estimated earnings date, this market will resolve to “No.” 

Note: Subsequent restatements, corrections, or revisions made to the initially announced GAAP EPS figure will not qualify for resolution, except in the case of obvious and immediate mistakes (e.g., fat finger errors, as with Lyft's (LYFT) earnings release in February 2024).
Note: The strike prices used in these markets are derived from SeekingAlpha estimates, and reflect the consensus of sell-side analyst estimates for GAAP EPS. 
Note: All figures will be rounded to the nearest cent using standard rounding.
Note: For the purposes of this market, IFRS EPS will be treated as GAAP EPS.
Note: For the purposes of this market, GAAP EPS refers to diluted GAAP EPS, unless this is not published, in which case it refers to basic GAAP EPS.
Note: All figures are expressed in USD, unless otherwise indicated.
Note: For primarily internationally listed companies, this market refers specifically to the shares traded in the United States on U.S. stock exchanges such as the NYSE or Nasdaq. In cases where the company trades in the U.S. through an American Depositary Receipt (ADR) or American Depositary Share (ADS), this market will refer to the ADR/ADS.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a slim 51% implied probability of Harley-Davidson (HOG) missing Q1 2026 earnings consensus at roughly $0.25 per share, reflecting caution after Q4 2025's earnings miss—posting a -$2.44 EPS versus expected -$1.01 amid 28% year-over-year revenue declines—and recent downward analyst revisions of 10.8% on quarterly revenue estimates. Persistent headwinds like layoffs, product recalls, and competitive pressures in the motorcycle segment have weighed on sentiment, with shares hovering near $18 versus analyst price targets around $23. The closely balanced odds hinge on the May 5 earnings release and accompanying new strategic plan, where beats on EPS, revenue near $1.03 billion, or upbeat 2026 guidance could decisively shift positioning.

markets.question: Will Iain Black win the 2026 Conservative Party of British Columbia leadership election?
markets.description:
The 2026 Conservative Party of British Columbia leadership election is currently scheduled to be held on May 30, 2026.

This market will resolve according to the candidate who becomes the next leader of the Conservative Party of British Columbia as a result of this election.

Temporary, interim, or placeholder leaders appointed before the election will not be considered.

If the result of this election isn't known by April 30, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other".

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting; however, if there is any ambiguity in the results, this market will resolve according to official information from the Conservative Party of British Columbia.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors former federal Conservative MP Kerry-Lynne Findlay at 37% implied probability to win the B.C. Conservative Party leadership via ranked-choice voting, reflecting her cabinet experience, endorsements from MLAs Sheldon Clare and Steve Kooner, and strong April 24 debate performance where she landed crowd-pleasing attacks on rivals while touting broad support. Caroline Elliott trails at 27%, buoyed by endorsements from MLAs Harman Bhangu and businessman Darrell Jones, though her clashes with Yuri Fulmer over social conservatism highlighted polarizing risks to second-choice votes. Peter Milobar's 15.5% share lags despite March polls showing him near Elliott, as his low-key debate style yielded less momentum amid membership verification underway ahead of May 23 online voting. Recent field narrowing to five after Warren Hamm's April 12 endorsement of Fulmer (now 10.8%) and party debt payoff underscore unity pressures.

markets.question: Will UBS or any of its underwriting affiliates serve as the lead underwriter in SpaceX’s initial public offering?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the bank that serves as the lead underwriter in the initial public offering of SpaceX.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or SpaceX completes an initial public offering without a designated lead underwriter, this market will resolve to “Other.”

If multiple banks are identified as lead underwriters, this market will resolve according to the primary lead underwriter. If the hierarchy between them is unclear, this market will resolve once it is conclusively evident which bank is the primary lead underwriter, for example, through the order in which the banks are listed in the underwriting section of SpaceX’s final initial public offering prospectus, once released.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official disclosures from SpaceX. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket positions Morgan Stanley as the frontrunner at 52% implied probability to serve as lead bank for SpaceX's anticipated "Project Apex" IPO, driven by banker Michael Grimes' longstanding ties to Elon Musk and the firm's leadership in the final bake-off presentation at Starbase. Recent Reuters and Financial Times reporting confirms SpaceX's confidential April 1 SEC filing for a potential $75 billion raise at over $1.75 trillion valuation, with Morgan Stanley, Bank of America (19%), Goldman Sachs (8%), JPMorgan, and Citigroup as active bookrunners among 21 banks. The Information highlighted ongoing jockeying between Morgan Stanley and Goldman for control, amid bridge financing led by Goldman. Traders eye an early June roadshow as the next catalyst amid shared senior roles.

markets.question: Will Mohamed Salah play in Scottish Premiership next?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the league of next club that Mohamed Salah officially joins by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET.

If Mohamed Salah does not officially join a new club by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Mohamed Salah signs a contract that includes a delayed transfer or loan-back clause requiring him to remain at Liverpool for a specified period before joining another club, this market will resolve to the club he is officially contracted to join following that period.

If Mohamed Salah joins a club in a league that is not listed, this market will resolve to “Other”.

If Mohamed Salah is released, retires, or is not under contract with any professional club by August 31, 2026 at 2:00 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

Transfers or assignments between a club’s senior team and reserve or affiliate teams will not be considered for this market.

An official transfer announcement prior to the market’s close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official announcements from Liverpool, Mohamed Salah and/or the acquiring club. A consensus of credible media reporting may also be used to resolve the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects intense uncertainty for Mohamed Salah's next league following Liverpool's March 24 confirmation of his free-agent departure at the end of the 2025-26 Premier League season, with top outcomes tightly clustered around 45-47% implied probability across Primeira Liga, Egyptian Premier League, Scottish Premiership, and La Liga. Egyptian national team media coordinator Muhammad Murad's April 28 revelation that Salah could announce within days—citing offers from Italy, France, Saudi Arabia, and other big clubs—has fueled the deadlock, as no frontrunner has emerged amid competing priorities of competitive legacy in Europe, lucrative Saudi Pro League deals from clubs like Al Ittihad, a potential Al Ahly homecoming, Rangers' reported internal discussions elevating Scottish Premiership chances, and Barcelona links for La Liga. Belgian Pro League interest lingers without specifics, while Premier League renewal odds at 10.5% underscore his Anfield exit finality, leaving traders weighing form, wages, and 2026 World Cup prep in this fluid transfer saga.

markets.question: Will ThreadGuy appear on the UpOnly podcast by December 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if a new episode of the UpOnly podcast featuring the specified guest is publicly released by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, it will resolve to “No.”

“Released” means the full episode is published and available to the general public on an official UpOnly platform (e.g. YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or the show’s official feed).

Previews, clips, teasers, or promotional material do not count toward resolution.

The resolution source will be the official UpOnly podcast channels and/or a consensus of credible public reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors One Nation's David Farley at 77% implied probability to win the Farrer by-election on May 9, reflecting his strong primary vote in recent polls around 31% and a pivotal preference deal from the Liberals and Nationals announced April 21, positioning him ahead of independent Michelle Milthorpe on 19%. This Coalition endorsement, prioritizing One Nation over the teal-backed Milthorpe amid rural voter concerns on water allocation and irrigation, has compressed her two-candidate-preferred lead from earlier surveys. Pre-polling commenced April 28 with queues forming, while a candidates' debate airs today; minor contenders like Liberal Raissa Butkowski trail far behind due to vote fragmentation under preferential voting. Outcomes hinge on preference flows and turnout in this traditionally conservative New South Wales riverina seat vacated by Sussan Ley.

markets.question: Will the UK’s 2026 inflation be at least 4.5%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in the U.K. over the 12-month period ending December 2026, before seasonal adjustment, as reported by the Office for National Statistics (ONS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026, according to the monthly ONS report.

The resolution source for this market will be the ONS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026 (https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/previousreleases), currently scheduled to be released on January 20, 2027. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

You can find this report by choosing the report for the relevant month from the previous releases page for price indices on the ONS website (https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/previousreleases). The relevant figure can be found in the "Consumer price inflation rates" table under the column labeled "CPI 12-month rate (%)" for the relevant month.

Note: the resolution source for this market will be the official monthly ONS CPI news release which reports inflation change over 12-month periods to only one decimal point (e.g. 1.9%). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market. For the full release schedule, see: https://www.ons.gov.uk/releasecalendar

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a narrow 51% implied probability for U.K. annual CPI inflation in 2026 falling in the 4.0-4.4% range, edging out 42.5% for 2.0-2.4%, reflecting uncertainty over whether March's headline CPI uptick to 3.3%—driven by energy and motor fuel surges amid Iran conflict tensions—signals persistent pressures or a transient shock. Core CPI eased slightly to 3.1%, supporting bets on mean reversion toward the Bank of England’s 2% target, while elevated food and services inflation risks, alongside surged public expectations to 4.5%, bolster higher bins. BoE forecasts now see averages near 3.2%, with rates held at 3.75%; April CPI and May Monetary Policy Report loom as key swing factors.

markets.question: Will Apple (AAPL) close above $190 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Apple (AAPL) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Apple (AAPL) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/AAPL/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The stock has gained about 7% through April, reflecting trader consensus on resilient services revenue growth countering modest iPhone unit sales amid China market headwinds and regulatory pressures. End-of-month rebalancing and pre-earnings volatility could sway the closing price near key technical levels like $271 resistance.

markets.question: Will Houston Texans win the 2027 NFL AFC Championship?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that wins the 2027 AFC championship game.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed team to win the 2027 NFL AFC championship per the rules of the NFL (e.g., they are eliminated in the playoffs), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2027 NFL AFC championship game is cancelled, postponed after March 31, 2027 ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from NFL (https://www.nfl.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on the 2027 AFC Champion market shows tight clustering among top contenders post-2026 NFL Draft, with Baltimore Ravens leading at 14.5% implied probability thanks to a B+ draft haul featuring Penn State guard Olaivavega Ioane at No. 14 to fortify the offensive line after free agency losses, plus edge rusher Zion Young, atop a proven roster anchored by Lamar Jackson. Buffalo Bills trail closely at 13% after an A-graded class emphasizing cornerbacks like Davison Igbinosun amid Josh Allen's elite play. Kansas City Chiefs at 10.5% added defensive talent including LSU CB Mansoor Delane at No. 6 despite trading Trent McDuffie, while surging New England Patriots (9%), Denver Broncos (9%), and Los Angeles Chargers (8.5%) benefit from young quarterbacks maturing—Drake Maye post-AFC Championship run, Bo Nix's second year, Justin Herbert under Jim Harbaugh—and targeted draft additions like OT Caleb Lomu and edge Akheem Mesidor, underscoring AFC-wide parity from distributed draft value and QB stability.

markets.question: Will OpenAI’s market cap be $1.5T or greater at market close on IPO day?
markets.description:
This market will resolve based on OpenAI's market capitalization at the closing price on its first day of trading.

If no IPO occurs by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "No IPO by December 31, 2026".

Market capitalization expresses the monetary value of a company’s outstanding shares, stated in its pricing currency. It is calculated as the number of shares outstanding multiplied by the closing share price on the first trading day.

If the relevant value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Resolution will be based on the primary exchange’s official listing page. In the event that the relevant figure is not displayed, another reliable source will be used.

In the event of an interruption in the course of the normal trading session on OpenAI’s first day of trading (e.g., a circuit breaker or half-day), the market will resolve according to the official closing price of the abbreviated session. If no such official closing price is published, the market will resolve according to the next trading day on which an official closing price is published, treating that as the first day of trading for purposes of this market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors no OpenAI IPO by December 31, 2026, at a 68.5% implied probability, driven by a Wall Street Journal report two days ago revealing missed revenue and user growth targets amid a high-stakes push toward a Q4 public offering. CFO Sarah Friar has privately warned of unsustainable $600 billion compute spending commitments—projected to burn $200 billion before cash flow—while clashing with CEO Sam Altman's aggressive timeline, leading to her exclusion from key investor meetings on server procurement. Fresh off a record $122 billion funding round valuing OpenAI privately at $852 billion, these operational shortfalls and organizational unreadiness temper enthusiasm for trillion-dollar public caps, with the highest bucket (1.25T–1.5T) at just 8%. Ongoing Elon Musk lawsuit over OpenAI's for-profit shift adds regulatory uncertainty, though an S-1 filing could shift odds rapidly.

markets.question: Will 200 to 229 tornadoes occur in the United States in April 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of tornadoes recorded in the United States during the specified month, based on the monthly count published on the National Centers for Environmental Information U.S. Tornadoes Time Series page (see: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/tornadoes/time-series).

Only tornadoes appearing in the final NCEI dataset for that month will count.

As of market creation, the relevant report is scheduled to be released on May 8, 2026, at 5:00 PM GMT+1 or 11:00 AM ET (Release schedule: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/dyk/monthly-releases). The market will resolve based on the first relevant tornado count published on the NCEI tornado time-series page after this scheduled release time.

If the value published after this scheduled release time is labeled preliminary, it will still determine resolution, and the market will resolve independently of any subsequent revisions, corrections, or retroactive adjustments. 

The market will not resolve based on any preliminary values published before the scheduled release time.

If no data is published by the scheduled release time, or if the NCEI website is temporarily unavailable, this market will remain open until that data is made available. If the relevant data is not made available by the date of the next scheduled publication ET, this market will resolve based on available data for the most recent prior month. If the NCEI website becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve using another credible source.

Only tornadoes appearing in the final NCEI dataset for that month will count.

As of market creation, the relevant report is scheduled to be released on May 8, 2026, at 5:00 PM GMT+1 or 11:00 AM ET (Release schedule: https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/access/monitoring/dyk/monthly-releases). The market will resolve based on the first relevant tornado count published on the NCEI tornado time-series page after this scheduled release time.

If the value published after this scheduled release time is labeled preliminary, it will still determine resolution, and the market will resolve independently of any subsequent revisions, corrections, or retroactive adjustments. 

The market will not resolve based on any preliminary values published before the scheduled release time.

If no data is published by the scheduled release time, or if the NCEI website is temporarily unavailable, this market will remain open until that data is made available. If the relevant data is not made available by the date of the next scheduled publication ET, this market will resolve based on available data for the most recent prior month. If the NCEI website becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve using another credible source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Preliminary Storm Prediction Center reports exceeding 300 tornadoes through April 27—driven by outbreaks like the April 17 Upper Midwest event (over 50 preliminary) and the April 23–28 Plains sequence featuring an EF4 near Enid, Oklahoma—have elevated trader consensus toward 260–350 totals, well above the 1991–2020 April average of 182. The narrow lead for 260–289 (54%) over 290–319 (45%) and 320–350 (46%) stems from uncertainty in ongoing National Weather Service surveys, which often downgrade weak reports to straight-line wind damage while confirming stronger vortices. Minimal severe risk lingered into April 29–30, limiting late additions; final National Centers for Environmental Information counts, due post-May 8, will resolve amid typical 10–20% adjustments from preliminaries.

markets.question: Ethereal FDV above $600M one day after launch?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Fully Diluted Valuation of Ethereal's governance token is greater than the value specified in the title 1 day after launch. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No."

The token must be actively, publicly transferable and tradable to be considered a launch.

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ethereal (https://x.com/etherealdex) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

The FDV will be determined using the total token supply multiplied by the token price.

"1 day after launch" is defined as 4:00 PM ET on the calendar day following launch. The resolution source for this market is the most liquid price source available. If Ethereal (https://x.com/etherealdex) doesn't launch a token by December 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Moscow be 12°C on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by NOAA at the Vnukovo International Airport in degrees Celsius on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=UUWW

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in short-range forecast models for Moscow's May 2 high, with 14°C (23.5%), 15°C (18.9%), and 16°C (16.3%) leading amid a recent Arctic blast that dumped heavy snow on April 27 and kept April 30 mornings near 1°C under overcast skies. South-westerly winds are now ushering milder Atlantic air, per Meteum.ai and Roshydromet-linked data showing afternoon peaks of 14°C with persistent cloud cover limiting solar heating, though some ensembles like world-weather.ru imply cooler 9°C if cold air lingers. Climatological early-May highs average 13–15°C; new GFS and ECMWF runs today and tomorrow could sharpen resolution as the event nears.

markets.question: Will the Democratic Party win the NM-01 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NM-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

Republicans cleared Ndidiamaka Okpareke, a Rio Rancho pharmacist who secured 85% delegate support at the March pre-primary convention, as their likely nominee after disqualifying stronger challengers like Jones. With no recent polling or catalysts shifting dynamics, the safe blue seat's historical incumbency advantage persists ahead of the November 3 general election. Late-breaking scandals, health issues, or a strong Republican national midterm wave could challenge this, though structural barriers remain high.

markets.question: Will SpaceX raise less than $40B in its IPO?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the total gross proceeds raised by SpaceX in its initial public offering (IPO) at the time of pricing.

The amount raised is defined as the aggregate dollar value of shares sold at the final offering price at the time of IPO pricing, as disclosed in the final prospectus filed with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission.

Shares sold pursuant to any overallotment option (greenshoe) will not be considered.

Secondary sales of existing shares by current shareholders will be included only to the extent that they are part of the IPO offering at pricing and reflected in the total gross proceeds. Private secondary transactions conducted outside of the IPO will not be considered.

If the total proceeds fall exactly on a boundary between two ranges, this market will resolve to the higher range.

If SpaceX does not complete an IPO by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to the lowest bracket.

The primary resolution source will be the final prospectus filed with the SEC; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

The April 1 confidential S-1 filing and April 6 banker meeting—led by Elon Musk and CFO Bret Johnsen—outlined a June roadshow, unprecedented 30% retail allocation, and Starlink-driven financials showing 24.8 billion dollars cash alongside heavy AI capex, but skepticism persists over executing at such a premium multiple given prior 800 billion dollar tender valuations and reported losses. Key swing factors include late-May prospectus details and market appetite for mega-IPOs, with Morgan Stanley and peers as lead underwriters.

markets.question: Nord Stream pipeline turned on before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any line of either Nord Stream 1 or Nord Stream 2 delivers natural gas to Germany or any EU member state in commercial quantities at any point by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

"Commercial quantities" refers to sustained, measurable flows of gas intended for end-user distribution. Test flows, pressure tests, or symbolic gas transfers not tied to actual supply operations will not count.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Nord Stream pipeline turned on before 2027?
events.description:

"Commercial quantities" refers to sustained, measurable flows of gas intended for end-user distribution. Test flows, pressure tests, or symbolic gas transfers not tied to actual supply operations will not count.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 92.5% on Nord Stream pipelines resuming operations before 2027, driven by ongoing Russia-Ukraine war tensions, EU sanctions, and Germany's firm policy shift away from Russian gas imports since 2022 sabotage. Recent insurer arguments in April 2026 classify the pipeline blasts as war-related damages, complicating potential repairs amid Nord Stream AG's insolvency proceedings. Despite speculative reports of US-Russia talks involving American investors and figures like Matthias Warnig, German officials reiterated no relaunch plans in mid-2025, with sabotage investigations—pointing to a pro-Ukrainian group and early CIA awareness—further entrenching legal and diplomatic barriers. Absent a Ukraine ceasefire or sanctions relief, traders see slim odds of commercial gas flows to Germany or EU states by year-end.

markets.question: Will Russia enter Ternuvate again by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Russia captures any territory of Ternuvate, Zaporizhzhya Oblast, (47.8301° N, 36.1268° E) between market creation and May 31, 2026, at 11:59 PM ET.

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Russian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Assessed Russian Infiltration Areas in Ukraine", “Assessed Russian Control”, “Assessed Russian Advance In Ukraine”, or “Assessed Russian Gains in the Past 24 Hours” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Russia comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded red in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Russia de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ternuvate, a small settlement on the Zaporizhzhia frontline, remains under Ukrainian control according to Institute for the Study of War (ISW) maps, with no verified Russian territorial gains since a brief February 2026 incursion that Ukrainian forces swiftly cleared. Recent Ukrainian counterattacks near Ternuvate, including southwest of Oleksandrivka as of early April, combined with Russian losses in the area prompting reinforcements toward Velyka Novosilka (April 27), have stabilized the sector amid ongoing positional fighting. Ukrainian advances in the Orikhiv direction (April 28 ISW assessment) further pressure Russian efforts. Trader consensus reflects the front's relative stasis despite broader Russian offensives elsewhere, with potential for escalation from troop redeployments or drone strikes, though no specific upcoming events are scheduled before May deadlines.

markets.question: Will Trump and Putin meet next in another EU country?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the location of the next meeting between Donald Trump and Vladimir Putin between September 30 and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve to "No meeting by June 30" if no qualifying meeting occurs during this market's timeframe.

A meeting is defined as any encounter where Putin and Trump are all present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

For this market, Gulf states are defined as the six members of the Gulf Cooperation Council (Bahrain, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Saudi Arabia, United Arab Emirates).

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

markets.question: Will Iran strike Dimona by April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran carries out a kinetic military strike on the listed facility between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This includes, but is not limited to, drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings. Cyber attacks, sanctions, or diplomatic actions will not count towards the resolution of this market.

For the purposes of this market, only strikes that cause physical damage to the infrastructure of the facility will be considered. Missile and drone strikes that are shot down or miss their targets, or make contact in open spaces or around the facility, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Cyber attacks will not qualify, even if they have kinetic effects.

events.title: What will Iran conduct military action against by April 30?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Iran carries out a kinetic military strike on the listed facility between market creation and April 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This includes, but is not limited to, drone and missile strikes, aerial bombings. Cyber attacks, sanctions, or diplomatic actions will not count towards the resolution of this market.

For the purposes of this market, only strikes that cause physical damage to the infrastructure of the facility will be considered. Missile and drone strikes that are shot down or miss their targets, or make contact in open spaces or around the facility, will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Only military actions by Iranian forces explicitly claimed by the Islamic Republic of Iran, or confirmed to have originated from Iranian territory, will count toward the resolution of this market. Attacks by proxy forces (i.e., Hezbollah, Houthis, etc.) will not count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

Note: Cyber attacks will not qualify, even if they have kinetic effects.

series.title: What will Iran conduct military action against

eventMetadata.context_description: Amid the ongoing 2026 Iran war, initiated by US-Israeli strikes on February 28 targeting Iranian missile sites and infrastructure, Tehran has retaliated with missile and drone attacks against Israel (southern cities like Arad), US regional bases, and Gulf allies including UAE and Kuwait (April 3 cruise missiles), Saudi Arabia (petrochemical complexes), and Bahrain. Iran's Army launched drone strikes on US-allied assets April 1, with air defenses activating April 23 amid vessel incidents in the Strait of Hormuz. No verified Iranian launches reported in the past week as ceasefire talks stall, Trump issues ultimatums, and US prepares potential escalation—shaping trader consensus on specific targets before midnight ET resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors former sports broadcaster Michele Tafoya at 78% implied probability to win the Minnesota Republican U.S. Senate primary on August 11, 2026, driven by her dominant 41% in a February Peak Insights poll among likely GOP primary voters—far ahead of Royce White at 11% and Adam Schwarze at 4%—and record Q1 fundraising of $2.04 million raised with $1.85 million cash on hand, outpacing rivals combined. National Republican Senatorial Committee support bolsters her amid a fragmented field of over 10 candidates. Recent district straw polls show Schwarze, a Navy SEAL veteran, surging with commanding leads, elevating his 15.2% odds ahead of the late-May state convention where endorsement often sways primaries, while rivals criticized Tafoya's April 7 debate absence. Raymond Petersen's steady grassroots backing holds him at 8.5%.

markets.question: Will Valtteri Bottas be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Mercedes' superior pace in the opening four rounds of the 2026 F1 season has positioned teammates George Russell and Kimi Antonelli as clear frontrunners for the Drivers' Championship, with Antonelli leading standings at 72 points to Russell's 63 after back-to-back victories in China and Japan. Trader consensus favors the more experienced Russell due to his qualifying dominance over Antonelli (22-5 head-to-head) and consistent podiums, including a win at the Australian GP, amid intra-team pressure labeled a "wake-up call" by pundits. Ferrari's Charles Leclerc (49 points) trails distantly in third, underscoring Mercedes' early constructors' lead and rivals' struggles with race pace and reliability.

events.title: Argentina vs. Algeria
events.description: This event is for the upcoming FIFA World Cup game, scheduled for Tuesday, June 16, 2026 between Argentina and Algeria.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending champions Argentina enter their FIFA World Cup 2026 Group J opener against Algeria on June 16 in Kansas City as 68.5% trader favorites, buoyed by superior squad depth and FIFA rankings despite mounting defensive injury concerns over the past two weeks. Key absences like Cristian Romero's season-ending knock, Emiliano Martínez's recent warm-up issue, and Lisandro Martínez's ongoing problems have tempered enthusiasm from higher implied probabilities, yet returns from Paulo Dybala and Lautaro Martínez bolster attacking options. Algeria, priced at 10%, faces a goalkeeper crisis with Luca Zidane out via facial fractures, backups Anthony Mandrea (shoulder surgery) and Melvin Mastil (groin hernia) sidelined, alongside Amine Gouiri's muscle strain, amplifying underdog status in this neutral-venue clash with no recent head-to-head since Argentina's 2007 friendly win.

markets.question: Will UK GDP growth in Q1 2026 be between 0% and 0.3%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the UK real gross domestic product (GDP) growth rate (% change) for Q1 of 2026 compared with the same quarter a year ago in the "GDP First quarterly estimate, UK" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for May 14, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/grossdomesticproductgdp/bulletins/gdpfirstquarterlyestimateuk/previousreleases

If no data for the specified quarter is released by the date the next quarter's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available quarter.

Note: data from the initial release of the referenced GDP report is what will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the initial release will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' near-even split between 1.2-1.5% (43%) and 0.6-0.9% (43%) implied probabilities for UK Q1 2026 GDP growth reflects robust January-February momentum—revised to +0.1% and +0.5% monthly, for a cumulative ~0.6%—offset by uncertainty over March data ahead of its May 14 ONS release. Strong services output (+0.5% three months to February) and manufacturing rebound (PMI at 53.6 in April) support upside potential, while construction's -2.0% three-month contraction caps gains. Broader forecasts like the OBR's 1.1% full-year growth temper exuberance, with resolution hinging on final quarterly aggregation versus Q4 2025's 0.1% pace.

markets.question: Will Africa win the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

For example, if France wins the tournament, the market will resolve to Europe.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The definitive source of the continent for each country will be World Population Review (https://worldpopulationreview.com/country-rankings/list-of-countries-by-continent).

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market will resolve to the continent of the country that wins the 2026 FIFA World Cup, currently scheduled for June 11-July 19, 2026.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is cancelled, postponed after December 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution sources for this market will be official information from FIFA (https://www.fifa.com/) and the World Population Review; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Europe at a dominant 72.5% implied probability to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, driven by unmatched depth across their 16 qualifiers—including powerhouses Spain, France, England, Portugal, Germany, Netherlands, and Croatia—that dominate post-qualification power rankings from ESPN, The Guardian, and FOX Sports. South America's 20.5% reflects the strength of six teams led by defending champions Argentina and Brazil, bolstered by Uruguay and Colombia's qualifier form. Asia (3.3%), Africa (3.1%), North America (2.3%), and Oceania (0.3%) trail due to fewer elite contenders despite expansions favoring debutants like Cape Verde, Curacao, Jordan, and Uzbekistan. Late March UEFA play-offs confirmed Bosnia and Herzegovina, Sweden, Turkey, and Czechia via penalty wins over Italy, Poland, Kosovo, and Denmark, reinforcing Europe's edge without elevating rivals, as intercontinental finals saw DR Congo and Iraq advance.

markets.question: LoL: NightBirds vs BRUTE - Game 2 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between NightBirds and BRUTE in the Hitpoint Masters Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 30 at 12:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "NightBirds" if NightBirds win Game 2 against BRUTE.

This market will resolve to "BRUTE" if BRUTE win Game 2 against NightBirds.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Game 2 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Game 2. 

If Game 2 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: LoL: NightBirds vs BRUTE (BO3) - Hitpoint Masters Regular Season
events.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between NightBirds and BRUTE in the Hitpoint Masters Regular Season, initially scheduled for April 30 at 12:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to "NightBirds" if NightBirds win the match against BRUTE.

This market will resolve to "BRUTE" if BRUTE win the match against NightBirds.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://gol.gg/esports/home. However, if https://gol.gg/esports/home has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: NightBirds hold a commanding edge as defending Hitpoint Masters Winter champions, having swept BRUTE 3-0 in the grand final and maintaining a perfect head-to-head record with multiple decisive wins, fueling trader consensus in their favor for this Spring regular season BO3. NightBirds sit undefeated at 2-0 (4-0 games) after 2-0 sweeps over Meavedron and Onion Team, while BRUTE languishes at 0-2 (0-4 games) following losses to CITA Kaizen and Meavedron. Both squads feature mid-April roster refreshes—NightBirds with new jungler Savero integrating seamlessly, BRUTE debuting an overhauled lineup including Interor top and Dunzi jungle—highlighting adaptation risks amid single round-robin pressure. No patch disruptions or sub status changes noted ahead of the April 30 matchup.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders' closely matched implied probabilities—48.8% for 0.0-0.5% and 46.5% for 0.5-1.0% year-over-year Q1 2026 GDP growth—hinge on INEGI's timely quarterly estimate due today, parsing weak monthly IGAE proxies amid manufacturing contraction. January's IGAE plunged 0.9% month-on-month with only 0.5% annual growth, while February eked out 0.1% MoM but contracted 0.3% YoY, pressured by softening U.S. demand, subdued industrial production, and March manufacturing PMI at contractionary 48.9. Differentiating factors include resilient services and remittances bolstering consumption versus persistent factory weakness; a March rebound could tip toward the higher bin, aligning with full-year forecasts near 1.6%.

markets.question: Will Microsoft (MSFT) close above $435 end of April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) on the final trading day of April 2026 is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If the final trading day of the month is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance — specifically, the Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/MSFT/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

markets.question: Will Trump name "Bernie" in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Donald Trump says the listed name between April 1, 2026, 12:00AM ET and April 30, 2026, 11:59PM ET. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

A ‘mention’ will include any verbal mention which is recorded (audio or video) and publicly accessible.

Written usages of terms (e.g. Truth Social posts, written statements, etc.) will not count toward this market's resolution.

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

Videos posted on his social media which were filmed outside this market's time frame will not qualify for a "Yes" resolution.

Cabinet shakeups—Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer's April 20 resignation and Navy Secretary John Phelan's April 22 departure—have spotlighted immigration hardliner Tom Homan (7%) and communications aide Karoline Leavitt (3%) as potential replacements, drawing Senate confirmation speculation.
