markets.question: Set Handicap: Ruud (-1.5) vs Blockx (+1.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Casper Ruud and Alexander Blockx in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for April 30, 2026 at 7:00AM ET.
This market will resolve to "Ruud" if Casper Ruud wins by 2 or more sets than Alexander Blockx, based on the final completed score. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Blockx."
If the match begins but is not completed, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins or delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a result, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.

events.title: Madrid Open: Casper Ruud vs Alexander Blockx
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Casper Ruud and Alexander Blockx in the Madrid Open, originally scheduled for April 29, 2026 at 7:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Casper Ruud' if Casper Ruud advances against Alexander Blockx.

This market will resolve to 'Alexander Blockx' if Alexander Blockx advances against Casper Ruud.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Defending Mutua Madrid Open champion Casper Ruud meets breakout 21-year-old Alexander Blockx in the ATP Masters 1000 quarterfinals on high-altitude clay. Ruud (No. 15), riding 2026 clay form of 8-1 entering this matchup after a grueling three-set survival over Stefanos Tsitsipas—saving two match points—leverages his elite baseline grinding, title defense pedigree, and perfect early-round wins over Munar and Davidovich Fokina. Blockx (No. 69), with a 12-3 clay surge this year, notched career-best upsets against Felix Auger-Aliassime and Francisco Cerundolo, powered by booming serves suiting Madrid's faster conditions. First head-to-head favors trader consensus on Ruud's experience, though Blockx's momentum and size (6-4) pose upset threat.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Will Ted Cruz post 200+ posts from April 28 to May 5, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Ted Cruz (@tedcruz), posts on X between April 28, 12:00 PM ET and May 5, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies which are recorded on the main feed will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

The resolution source for this market is the "Post Counter" figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices 60-79 posts by Ted Cruz (@tedcruz) at 61% implied probability for the April 24-May 1 window (12:00 PM ET), reflecting his moderated 8-10 daily pace amid a quieter Senate calendar lacking prior catalysts like border security debates that drove 100-119 posts April 21-28 and 160-179 April 17-24. With six days elapsed as of April 30, steady output covers FBI raids on Somali-linked fraud in Minneapolis, King Charles III's joint session address, Schumer's ICE remarks, podcast promotions, and memes, projecting toward the low 60s without surges. The 80-99 range at 30% accounts for potential weekend upticks, while higher bins fade absent major events. Resolution via XTracker post counter.

markets.question: Will New England Patriots win the 2026 AFC East?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the team that is declared as the AFC East division champion for the 2026 NFL regular season.

If multiple teams tie atop the division, this market will resolve according to the official tiebreak procedure of the NFL.

If the 2026 NFL regular season is cancelled, postponed after January 17, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, or there is otherwise no winner declared for this division within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the NFL; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

markets.question: Will the lowest temperature in Miami be between 82-83°F on May 2?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the lowest temperature recorded at the Miami Intl Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 2 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the lowest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Miami Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/fl/miami/KMIA.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus reflects high uncertainty in the May 2 overnight low at Miami International Airport, with market-implied probabilities tightly clustered across 71–81°F bins due to divergent National Weather Service guidance and ensemble model runs from GFS and ECMWF. A weak low pressure system tracking from the Gulf of Mexico on May 1–2 introduces variability in cloud cover and low-level winds; clearer skies with light southeast flow would enhance radiative cooling toward 71°F or below or 74–75°F, while increased pre-frontal moisture and clouds favor warmer 76–81°F outcomes by trapping heat. Persistent ridge aloft has delivered recent lows near 71°F under similar clear conditions, but historical May averages hover around 76°F amid urban heat island effects and warm ocean influences. Watch for afternoon NWS updates and 12Z model refreshes resolving shortwave positioning.

markets.question: Series: Anyone's Legend Kill Handicap (-10.5) vs Weibo Gaming (+10.5)
markets.description:
This market refers to the LoL match between Anyone's Legend and Weibo Gaming in the LPL Group Ascend, scheduled for January 20 at 4:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match. 

Otherwise, it will resolve to "Weibo Gaming".

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any Federal or State jurisdiction of the United States formally charges or otherwise announces a criminal indictment of Leslie Wexner between market creation and June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices "No" at 85% on Les Wexner facing criminal charges by June 30, reflecting the absence of any DOJ or FBI indictment actions despite heightened scrutiny in the Jeffrey Epstein probe. Wexner's February 2026 House Oversight Committee deposition, where he denied knowledge of Epstein's crimes and claimed to have been duped, yielded no new criminal referrals, echoing 2019 subpoenas resolved via attorney statements without direct interviews. Recent Epstein files naming Wexner alongside co-conspirators like Ghislaine Maxwell raised civil suits from victims in April, but experts note these do not indicate prosecutable evidence. With no grand jury activity or charges announced amid ongoing congressional inquiries, traders view barriers to federal prosecution as substantial ahead of the deadline.

markets.question: Will both candidates advancing to the general election for Governor of California from the June 2, 2026 primary be affiliated with the Republican Party?
markets.description:
The non-partisan primary election for Governor of California is scheduled to take place on June 2, 2026. The top two candidates in this election by number of votes won will advance to the general election for Governor of California.

This market will resolve according to the party affiliation of the listed candidates who advance from the primary to the general election for Governor of California. A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with a party.

In case any combination of parties advances that is not listed here, including all scenarios where an independent candidate advances, this market will resolve to "Other".

If no 2026 California gubernatorial primary takes place by December 31, 2026, this market will resolve to “Other.”

This market will resolve based on the results of the primary election for Governor of California as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the government of California, specifically the Office of the Secretary of State.

Eric Swalwell's mid-April withdrawal has consolidated Democratic votes without propelling any single contender to overtake Hilton, while high undecideds (23-26%) and California's Democratic voter registration edge diminish Dem-Dem (18%) and Rep-Rep (5.9%) chances. Post-debate surveys from late April reinforce this positioning, with a CNN debate on May 5 ahead of the June primary potentially tipping the second-place battle.

markets.question: Will US GDP growth in Q1 2026 be greater than 3.5%?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the seasonally adjusted and annualized GDP "Advance Estimate" release for Q1 of 2026, scheduled for April 30, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The GDP release will be made available here: https://www.bea.gov/data/gdp/gross-domestic-product

Note: data in the first available GDP report is labelled by the BEA as an "Advance Estimate". The data found in the advance estimate will be used to resolve this market. Data may be revised during the following quarter or as a part of the next estimate's publication, however any revisions to GDP report data made after the release of the advance estimate will not be considered for this market's resolution.

If the advance estimate is not released, this market will resolve based on the first officially published figure for real GDP for the specified quarter (e.g., the ‘second’ or ‘third’ estimate, etc.), as reported by the BEA. If no official estimate is released by the date the next quarter's advanced estimate is scheduled to be published, this market will resolve based on the most recent previous figure released by the BEA.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders assign a 46.1% implied probability to US real GDP growth of 2.0–2.5% in Q1 2026, reflecting economist consensus forecasts around 2.3% annualized and a rebound from Q4 2025's subdued 0.5% pace. This positioning stems from resilient consumer spending, highlighted by March retail sales surging 1.7% month-over-month—the largest advance in over three years—alongside ISM manufacturing PMI expansion to 52.7 and nonfarm payrolls adding 178,000 jobs. However, the Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model at 1.2% as of April 29 tempers optimism, boosting probabilities for 1.0–1.5% (10%). The BEA advance estimate, due today at 8:30 AM ET alongside March personal income and outlays, represents the key resolution catalyst.

markets.question: PEC Zwolle vs. Heracles Almelo: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game between PEC Zwolle and Heracles Almelo, scheduled for May 3 at 8:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if PEC Zwolle and Heracles Almelo combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

Both sides endured heavy defeats on April 5—Zwolle thrashed 5-0 by Go Ahead Eagles and Heracles routed 4-1 at Heerenveen—highlighting porous defenses amid Heracles' desperate survival fight. Zwolle benefit from home form and mid-table comfort, while Heracles eye revenge after their stunning 8-2 win over Zwolle in November 2025; key absences like Zwolle's Shola Shoretire (knee, out until early May) could influence lineups, with table positions and recent momentum shaping trader consensus on outcomes like win, goals, or clean sheets.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects ECB-Fed policy divergence as the key driver for EUR/USD's 2026 trajectory, with the pair trading near 1.166 amid a recent slip below 1.17 on broad USD strength. Eurozone inflation accelerated to 2.6% in March 2026—up from February's 1.9%—bolstering expectations of ECB rates holding steady at today's April 30 Governing Council meeting, while markets price Fed funds cuts later this year amid softer U.S. data. This 160 basis point rate gap favors euro upside potential, though sticky services inflation and fiscal risks could cap gains. Watch June ECB/Fed meetings and May CPI releases for shifts in the implied rate path.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 1.25% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%–3.75% following its April 28–29, 2026 FOMC meeting, a divisive decision amid March CPI inflation accelerating to 3.3% year-over-year—the highest since May 2024—prompting policymakers to emphasize data-dependent caution on rate cuts. This reflects trader consensus on persistent inflationary pressures from energy prices and services, offsetting labor market resilience with unemployment projected at 4.4% for Q4 2026 per the March dot plot, which anticipates gradual easing to around 3.00%–3.25% by end-2027. Polymarket odds capture this skin-in-the-game sentiment, with upcoming catalysts including April CPI on May 12 and the June 16–17 FOMC potentially tipping expectations for the first 25-basis-point cut.

markets.question: Will DeepSeek have the #3 AI model at the end of April 2026 (Style Control On)?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

events.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the third-highest arena rank (Style Control On) based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab is checked on April 30, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Overall" Leaderboard tab at https://lmarena.ai/leaderboard/text with style control on will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies third place under this ranking system.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors Anthropic at 97.1% implied probability for holding the #3 large language model on the LMArena Chatbot Arena Text leaderboard (Style Control On variant) as of April 30, driven by Claude Opus 4.7's mid-April 16 release, which rapidly climbed to secure that position amid intense battles. This follows Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview dominating #1 and OpenAI's GPT-5.5 claiming #2, with significant ELO separation underscoring Claude Opus 4.7's edge in style-controlled text generation tasks like creative writing and instruction-following. No major leaderboard shifts have occurred in the past week despite releases like DeepSeek V4, reflecting stable human preference data. Realistic challenges include a surprise model drop or late battle influx before resolution, though low odds reflect traders' confidence in the current pecking order.

markets.question: Elon Bull Run Parlay
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if all of the following conditions are met between January 1 and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET:

- Elon Musk trillionaire
- Another Elon baby
- 9+ SpaceX Starship launches reach space

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will remain open until it is confirmed that at least one of the above conditions has not been met, or until all of the above conditions have been met within the specified timeframe.

The full rules for this market can be found here: 
https://polymarket-upload.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/Elon+Bull+Run+Parlay.pdf

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices "No" at 88% for the Elon Bull Run Parlay, reflecting deep skepticism that all three 2026 conditions—Elon Musk reaching trillionaire status per Bloomberg Billionaires Index, announcing a 15th child, and SpaceX achieving 9+ Starship launches reaching 62-mile altitude—will occur. Tesla's Q1 earnings on April 24 beat EPS expectations at $0.41 but triggered a muted stock response amid softening EV demand and intensifying Chinese competition, leaving Musk's net worth around $800-850 billion despite February's SpaceX-xAI merger valuations topping $1.25 trillion combined. Starship Flight 12 faces May delays with zero qualifying launches year-to-date, underscoring cadence challenges, while no credible reports of a new baby exist. Upcoming Q2 deliveries and Starship tests could nudge odds, but the parlay's multiplicative improbability dominates sentiment.

markets.question: Will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit (HIGH) $100 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during April 2026, any 1-minute candle for Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.HOOD%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit in April 2026?
events.description: What will Robinhood Markets, Inc. (HOOD) hit in April 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Robinhood Markets (HOOD) shares traded around $71 on April 30, 2026, down sharply from recent highs after Q1 earnings on April 28 revealed total net revenues of $1.07 billion—up 15% year-over-year but below the $1.14 billion consensus estimate—driven by a 47% plunge in crypto trading revenue to $134 million amid subdued Bitcoin prices. Strong net deposits of $18 billion and a 320% surge in prediction markets revenue to $147 million highlighted diversification and user engagement, with diluted EPS at $0.38. Analyst median price targets stand at $100, balancing fintech growth against crypto exposure. With month-end resolution imminent, intraday volatility and closing levels near $70 support will finalize trader positioning.

markets.question: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 1.5% and 1.7%?
markets.description:
This is a market about the variation of consumer prices in South Korea in the 12-month period ending December 2026, as reported by the South Korean Ministry of Data and Statistics (MODS).

This market will resolve according to the percentage change in South Korea’s Consumer Price Index (CPI) over the 12-month period ending December 2026 (December CPI % change compared to the same month of the previous year), according to the monthly MODS Consumer Price Survey report for the specified month.

The resolution source for this market will be the MODS Consumer Price Index monthly report released for December 2026, currently scheduled to be released on December 31, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon release of the aforementioned data. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The relevant report will be made available upon release at: https://mods.go.kr/board.es?mid=a20109020000&bid=11751&eng_board_type=01

Note: This market’s resolution source reports percentage change in the South Korean Consumer Price Index to only one decimal point (e.g. 2.0%). Thus this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving this market. For the full release schedule, see: https://mods.go.kr/schdl.es?mid=a20301000000

eventMetadata.context_description: **Trader consensus on Polymarket prices South Korea's 2026 annual CPI inflation above 3.0% at a 51% implied probability, narrowly ahead of the 2.1%-2.3% band at 43.4%, reflecting heightened uncertainty amid accelerating price pressures.** The Bank of Korea's April 10 policy statement flagged headline inflation exceeding its prior 2.2% forecast "considerably," driven by surging oil prices and won depreciation, following March's 2.2% year-over-year CPI rise—the highest since December—from 2.0% in prior months. Core inflation held near 2.3%-2.5%, supporting BOK's 2% target yet signaling upside risks from energy costs and producer prices at a three-year high of 4.1%. Key differentiators include April CPI data due early May, wage growth, and global commodity trends, with closely matched odds underscoring traders' debate over sustained reacceleration versus moderating demand-side forces.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 220-239 tweets from May 1 to May 8, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X from May 1 12:00 PM ET to May 8, 2026 12:00 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Former U.S. Rep. Ben McAdams holds a commanding trader consensus at 71% implied probability for the UT-01 Democratic primary on June 23, driven by his dominant fundraising—over $1.5 million raised and $863,000 cash on hand as of early April, dwarfing Nate Blouin's $526,000—and a late-March Data for Progress poll showing him at 36% among likely primary voters to Blouin's 23%. Kathleen Riebe's April 11 dropout and endorsement of McAdams, citing Blouin's "volatile" style, further consolidated moderate support. Despite Liban Mohamed's April 25 state convention upset via ranked-choice delegate voting, traders discount activist-driven outcomes in favor of McAdams' congressional experience and broader voter appeal in the newly redrawn, Democratic-leaning district.

markets.question: Will Qinwen Zheng win the 2026 Women’s US Open?
markets.description:
The 2026 U.S. Open tennis tournament is scheduled for August 23 - September 13, 2026.

This market will resolve to the player that wins the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed player to win the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament per the rules of the tournament, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the 2026 U.S. Open Women’s Singles Tournament is cancelled, postponed after October 31, 2026, or there is otherwise no winner declared within that timeframe, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the U.S. Open (https://www.usopen.org/index.html); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus positions Aryna Sabalenka as the clear US Open favorite at 35.5% implied probability, driven by her world No. 1 ranking, near-perfect 25-1 record through late April 2026, and a deep run to the Madrid Open fourth round on clay, underscoring her hard-court dominance as the 2024 champion. Elena Rybakina's 18.7% follows her recent Porsche Tennis Grand Prix title in Stuttgart—edging Karolina Muchova in the final—and Australian Open major win earlier this year, despite Madrid fourth-round exit and lingering injury concerns. Iga Swiatek lags at 16.0% amid a Madrid retirement due to illness and ongoing hard-court inconsistencies, contrasting her clay prowess. Xiyu Wang's surprising 11.1% reflects breakout momentum in recent ITF events, while Coco Gauff and others benefit from home-crowd edge and rankings stability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ralph Alvarado leads trader consensus at 80.5% implied probability to win Kentucky's 6th Congressional District Republican primary on May 19, driven by his fundraising dominance alongside Greg Plucinski and a pivotal U.S. Chamber of Commerce endorsement announced April 29, signaling strong establishment support in the open seat race vacated by Andy Barr's Senate bid. As former state senator, Alvarado benefits from name recognition and conservative credentials highlighted in recent debates against Ryan Dotson, positioning him as the frontrunner. Challengers like Dotson (10.3%), Gavin Solomon (9.9%), Adam Perez Arquette (7.0%), and Plucinski (3.4%) trail amid limited polling, with late endorsements or debate performances potentially shifting dynamics in this closed primary.
